2606.28697.title: Mitigating Batch Effects in Histopathology via Language-Mediated Robust Embedding Generation
2606.28697.paper_summary: Pathology foundation models (PFMs) have demonstrated strong potential across clinical and scientific applications, yet their performance is often hindered by batch effects, which are non-biological variations across tissue source institutions (TSIs) that distort learned feature representations and impair generalization. Conventional mitigation strategies, such as stain normalization, offer limited success in addressing these high-dimensional, complex artifacts. We present GLMP (General-purpose LLM-Mediated Pathology model), a novel framework that generates robust numerical embeddings from histology image patches through an intermediate textual representation. By leveraging pretrained general-purpose multimodal large language models (MLLMs) and text encoders, GLMP effectively prioritizes biologically meaningful signals over TSI-specific artifacts, thereby improving cross-institutional generalization. To our knowledge, GLMP is the first pathology model to use text descriptions of histological features as an intermediate representation for generating numerical embeddings from histology images. Our results highlight the untapped potential of broad-domain, non-specialized MLLMs in computational pathology and introduce a new paradigm for building versatile, generalizable, and robust pathology models.
2606.28697.paper_summary_zh: 病理基礎模型（PFMs）在臨床和科學應用中顯示出強大的潛力，但它們的表現常常受到批次效應的阻礙，這些效應是來自組織來源機構（TSIs）之間的非生物變異，扭曲了學習到的特徵表示並損害了泛化能力。傳統的緩解策略，如染色標準化，在解決這些高維度、複雜的伪影方面效果有限。我們提出了GLMP（通用LLM介導的病理模型），這是一個新穎的框架，通過中間文本表示從組織學圖像片段生成穩健的數值嵌入。通過利用預訓練的通用多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）和文本編碼器，GLMP有效地優先考慮生物學上有意義的信號，而非TSI特定的伪影，從而改善跨機構的泛化能力。據我們所知，GLMP是第一個使用組織學特徵文本描述作為中間表示，從組織學圖像生成數值嵌入的病理模型。我們的結果突顯了廣域、非專業化MLLMs在計算病理學中的未開發潛力，並引入了一種建立多功能、可泛化和穩健病理模型的新範式。
2606.28697.author: Yishu Zhang et.al.
2606.28697.authors: Yishu Zhang, Shushan Wu, Zhenzhong Zhang, Didong Li, Huaxiu Yao, Yun Li, Iain Carmichael, Katherine A. Hoadley, Hongtu Zhu, Di Wu, Daiwei Zhang
